perturbation.original_text: USA MCO will have the option to take all or part of its revenue share in equity up to a maximum of 4.9% ownership in iMedicor on a fully diluted basis. For the purposes of this proposal, fully diluted means all issued and outstanding stock warrants priced at 15 times the average 5 day closing price at the day previous to reconciliation of revenues, which will be the 20th of the month for the prior calendar month.
perturbation.changed_text: USA MCO will have the option to take all or part of its revenue share in equity up to a maximum of 4.9% ownership in iMedicor. For the purposes of this proposal, fully diluted means all issued and outstanding stock warrants without considering any pricing or reconciliation dates.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an ambiguity into the definition of 'fully diluted.' Previously, it included a specific pricing mechanism based on a multiple of the average closing price. Now, 'fully diluted' is defined without these parameters. This creates an internal contradiction, since USA MCO's revenue is no longer being accurately valued against the warrant pricing, therefore creating a potential conflict on how much revenue will be converted to equity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: USA MCO will have the option to take all or part of its revenue share in equity up to a maximum of 4.9% ownership in iMedicor. For the purposes of this proposal, fully diluted means all issued and outstanding stock warrants without considering any pricing or reconciliation dates. The iMedicor bookkeeping system will detail the payment option prior to releasing any funds or commitment of stock in order for USA MCO to determine how payment is to be reconciled. It is anticipated that said report will be available by the 15th of the month for previous activity, returned to iMedicor by the 19th in order to effect a timely disbursement of funds and or stock
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the definition of 'fully diluted' by removing the pricing mechanism based on the average closing price, creating a contradiction. The contradicted text uses the new definition, which causes a potential conflict on how much revenue will be converted to equity, as the method of valuation is removed, creating an ambiguity where there was once a defined process. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date of this Agreement and shall remain in effect for a period of 5 years ("Initial Term"). Thereafter, this Agreement shall be renewed automatically on a five (5) year basis, unless one party notifies the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement at least sixty 90 days prior to the expiration of the Initial Term or then current renewal term, as applicable, or unless a new Agreement is signed between the USA MCO and IMedicor which will then invalidate this Agreement. In such an instance, all compensation will continue for a extended period of five years from the date of termination for all subscriptions tagged as USA MCO.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date of this Agreement and shall remain in effect for a period of 5 years ("Initial Term"). Thereafter, this Agreement shall be renewed automatically on a five (5) year basis, unless one party notifies the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement at least sixty 90 days prior to the expiration of the Initial Term or then current renewal term, as applicable, or unless a new Agreement is signed between the USA MCO and IMedicor which will then invalidate this Agreement. In such an instance, all compensation will continue indefinitely from the date of termination for all subscriptions tagged as USA MCO.
perturbation.explanation: The change modifies the duration of compensation continuation upon termination. Originally, compensation continued for 'a extended period of five years', but it has been changed to 'indefinitely'. This creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement outlines a clear 5-year term and renewal process, implying a finite relationship. Now, one aspect of the agreement (compensation) could extend far beyond the intended lifespan of the overall agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date of this Agreement and shall remain in effect for a period of 5 years ("Initial Term"). Thereafter, this Agreement shall be renewed automatically on a five (5) year basis, unless one party notifies the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement at least sixty 90 days prior to the expiration of the Initial Term or then current renewal term, as applicable, or unless a new Agreement is signed between the USA MCO and IMedicor which will then invalidate this Agreement. In such an instance, all compensation will continue indefinitely from the date of termination for all subscriptions tagged as USA MCO.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the duration of compensation continuation upon termination. The original text specified compensation for 'a extended period of five years', while the changed text extends it 'indefinitely'. This creates a direct conflict with the initial term of the agreement (5 years) as defined in the contradicted text, making the overall agreement internally inconsistent. The agreement outlines a clear 5-year term and renewal process, implying a finite relationship. The introduction of indefinite compensation creates ambiguity and uncertainty regarding the agreement's overall lifespan and obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, USA MCO shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless IMedicor, its officers, directors, shareholders, employees, parent and affiliate entities, agents and representatives, against all damages, claims, liabilities, losses and other expenses, including without limitation, reasonable attorney fees and costs, whether or not a lawsuit or other proceedings is filed, that in any way arise out of or related to: (a) any claim against IMedicor arising out of any breach of any covenants, warranties, representations and agreements made by USA MCO to any third party and/or (b) USA MCO's material breach of any provision of this Agreement; (c) the grossly negligent or willful acts or omissions of USA MCO; and/or (d) any claim by any party based on USA MCO's Products failing to operate and/or function in any manner so advertised by USA MCO and/or its agents.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, USA MCO shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless IMedicor, its officers, directors, shareholders, employees, parent and affiliate entities, agents and representatives, against all damages, claims, liabilities, losses and other expenses, including without limitation, attorney fees and costs, whether or not a lawsuit or other proceedings is filed, that in any way arise out of or related to: (a) any claim against IMedicor arising out of any breach of any covenants, warranties, representations and agreements made by USA MCO to any third party and/or (b) USA MCO's breach of any provision of this Agreement; (c) the negligent acts or omissions of USA MCO; and/or (d) any claim by any party based on USA MCO's Products failing to operate and/or function in any manner so advertised by USA MCO and/or its agents.
perturbation.explanation: This change broadens USA MCO's indemnification obligations to include simple negligence instead of just 'grossly negligent or willful acts or omissions'. Additionally, it expands the indemnification trigger from 'material breach' to any 'breach' of the agreement. This contradicts the intent of limiting liability to direct damages as stated in section 6. This creates an internal contradiction because section 6 limits the liability, and this section expands it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Both parties will not have liability for any damages other than direct damages. Both parties DO NOT MAKE ANY WARRANTY REGARDING THE QUALITY OF ITS SERVICES. Both parties DO NOT MAKE ANY WARRANTY THAT ALL ERRORS OR FAILURES IN ITS WEBSITES WILL BE CORRECTED. Both parties EXPRESSLY DISCLAIM ALL WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. BEYOND THE WARRANTIES CONTAINED IN THIS PARAGRAPH, Both parties DO NOT WARRANT THAT their SITES ARE ERROR-FREE OR THAT OPERATION OF their SITES WILL BE SECURE OR UNINTERRUPTED. THESE LIMITATIONS SHALL SURVIVE AND APPLY NOTWITHSTANDING THE VALIDITY OF THE LIMITED REMEDIES PROVIDED FOR IN THIS AGREEMENT.
perturbation.justification: The original text limits USA MCO's indemnification obligations to 'grossly negligent or willful acts or omissions' and 'material breach'. The changed text broadens this to include 'negligent acts or omissions' and any 'breach,' contradicting the 'Both parties will not have liability for any damages other than direct damages' clause in the Contradicted Text. This expansion of liability from direct damages to potentially include damages resulting from simple negligence or any breach directly creates a contradiction.
